classObjective: Write two-column proofs using the Isosceles Triangle Theorem and its converse.
workedExamples: 14-6, Example 1 — Write Two-Column Proofs Using Isosceles Triangle Properties
14-6, Example 2 — Find Side Lengths and Angle Measures in Isosceles Triangles
warmUp: What does the Isosceles Triangle Theorem state?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: Isosceles Triangle Theorem: congruent sides → congruent angles; converse: congruent angles → congruent sides; proof applications
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice applying Isosceles Triangle Theorem and its converse in proofs
independentPractice: Practice problems using isosceles triangle properties in angle and side proofs
assessment: Exit ticket: use the Isosceles Triangle Theorem to find all angles of an isosceles triangle with vertex angle 40°
capReflection: How does the converse of the Isosceles Triangle Theorem allow you to prove sides are congruent from angle information?
